calendar.matters:
PETITION TO ESTABLISH FACT OF MARRIAGE
Off Calendar Feb-18-2025 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Huy Dinh Quach, Petitioner.
Hung Thi Hong, wife to Petitioner.
Steven Quach, son to Petitioner.

The court noted the petition lacked essential information. The petitioners described their limited English proficiency at the time and their belief that the marriage was valid, given the official paperwork they received and their consistent filing of taxes as a married couple for over four decades. The issue arose recently when the husband attempted to retire and was informed that the marriage certificate lacked a required witness signature, which could affect his wife's eligibility for spousal retirement benefits. The petitioner presented documentation, including photographs from the wedding. After review, the court confirmed that the marriage date was consistently recognized as October 12, 1982.

The court found sufficient evidence that the petitioners entered into a valid marriage on October 12, 1982 and granted the petition. Order signed in open court.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)
